text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet little town nestled between rolling hills and dense forests, there was a school called Oakwood Academy. The building stood tall with its ivy-covered walls, a venerable institution steeped in the history of generations of students and teachers.

It was a storybook day at Oakwood when young Anna decided to bake bread. Her grandmother's famous loaf recipe had been handed down through the women of the family, a secret ingredient involving a dash of magic – not necessarily magical, but certainly mysterious.

Anna kneaded the dough, the warmth of the sun against her cheeks as she hummed a tune. After letting it rise in the oven's crevice until it doubled in size, she slapped it down onto the baking sheet and brushed it with honey. In a burst of nostalgia, she sang a verse from her grandmother's favorite folk song.

Hours passed and the bread sat, golden-brown and inviting. As Anna walked home through the crisp air, she felt a curious
